element.name: TCSVImporter
element.short: CSV and TSV file importer implementation.

descr.p: TCSVImporter is a concrete importer class capable of reading comma-separated (CSV) and tab-separated (TSV) text files. It parses the input stream according to the delimiter, quoting rules, encoding, and trimming options specified in TImportOptions, producing TXRow objects that are then processed by the associated TFieldMapper.
This importer is typically registered under the .csv and .tsv extensions in TImporterRegistry.

element.name: TCSVImporter.Probe
element.short: Attempts to detect whether the input stream represents a CSV or TSV file.

descr.p: Analyzes the file name and a small portion of the stream to determine whether the content resembles CSV or TSV data. The method returns a score indicating the likelihood that this importer can handle the file. Higher scores represent a better match.
The registry uses this score when selecting the most appropriate importer for a given file.

element.name: TCSVImporter.CanHandleExtension
element.short: Returns True if the importer supports the given file extension.

descr.p: Checks whether the extension corresponds to a CSV or TSV file. This is a fast preliminary filter used before probing the file contents.

element.name: TCSVImporter.Import
element.short: Reads and processes all rows from a CSV or TSV stream.

descr.p: Parses the input stream according to the delimiter, quoting rules, encoding, and trimming options provided in TImportOptions. Each parsed row is converted into a TXRow and passed through the TFieldMapper before being emitted to the RowOut callback.
The method supports progress reporting and cancellation via Options.OnProgress and Options.Cancel.

element.name: TCSVImporter.GetFieldNames
element.short: Extracts the list of column names from the CSV/TSV header.

descr.p: Reads the first line of the input stream and returns a TStringList containing the field names. If Options.HasHeader is False, the importer generates synthetic field names such as Column1, Column2, etc.

element.name: TCSVImporter.PreviewRows
element.short: Reads a limited number of rows for preview purposes.

descr.p: Parses up to MaxRows rows from the CSV/TSV stream and emits them through the RowOut callback. This is used by the import wizard to display sample data before the full import begins.
The preview respects all parsing options, including delimiters, quoting, trimming, and header handling.
